response.justification: The candidate is technically an excellent fit for the stack — strong TypeScript/Node.js, React, Kubernetes, and even the nice-to-have LLM/MCP and AI-agent experience — but he is a staff/senior engineer with nearly two decades of experience applying to an explicitly entry-level position. The overqualification raises serious concerns about salary alignment, engagement, and retention, and his Bucharest location conflicts with the high on-site presence requirement. Worth a conversation only if compensation expectations and relocation/on-site availability are clarified upfront.
strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js and React experience across multiple product companies
Hands-on LLM, MCP, and AI-agent tooling experience matching nice-to-have qualifications
Strong DevOps breadth including Kubernetes, Docker, and CI/CD craftsmanship (TDD, trunk-based development)
concerns: Heavily overqualified for an entry-level role — retention, salary, and motivation risk
Based in Bucharest while the role requires high on-site presence
No explicit PostgreSQL experience (MongoDB and MySQL listed instead); Nest.js not evidenced

key_factors.factor: Seniority mismatch (senior applying to junior role)
Tech stack alignment (TypeScript, React, Node, LLM/AI)
